The Eternal Circuit — 81 Mainline Cities — Perpetual Passage

The Prussian Star

The last civilised address in the world

In the spring of 1972, on the twentieth anniversary of the company's founding, Wilhelm Global Holdings launched what would become the most ambitious passenger rail operation in human history. The Prussian Star — eighty-two carriages of steel, mahogany, and purpose — departed Berlin on her first full global circuit, carrying over two thousand passengers across eighty-one cities on six continents.

She does not stop. She does not slow. Built in the late 1950s and comprehensively refitted in 1969, she has run without pause since that March departure — through blizzard and desert, across frozen steppe and equatorial plain. Aboard her, the finest traditions of European hospitality continue uninterrupted, three hundred and sixty-five days a year.

Three classes. Three worlds. One eternal train.

Passenger Classes

Choose your passage

Every class boards the same eternal train. What differs is the world you inhabit within it.

Third Class

3rd Class

The warmth of survival

  • Shared berth accommodations, 6 per cabin
  • Access to communal dining hall, two sittings
  • Ration allotment plus supplementary canteen
  • Heated corridor access between daylight hours
  • Laundry facilities — twice weekly
  • Medical station access, appointments required
One-Way Passage — Final Sale Free

Requires membership in the Wilhelm Community on Roblox. No return — the world outside does not permit it.

Join Community & Board
Second Class

2nd Class

Comfort above the frost

  • Private compartment with fold-down berths
  • À la carte dining in the Scarlet Dining Car
  • Access to the Observation Lounge (restricted hours)
  • Daily linen service and turndown
  • Personal porter assignment, shared
  • Library and reading room access
  • Weekly bathing allocation — private schedule
One-Way Passage — Final Sale 600 R
First Class

1st Class

The world as it was — and ever shall be

  • Private suite with en-suite bathing chamber
  • Dedicated personal steward, available at all hours
  • Exclusive Dining Room — seven-course suppers
  • Private observation dome — unobstructed panoramas
  • The Wilhelm Spa — mineral baths and treatment rooms
  • Access to the Grand Salon and cognac library
  • Telegraph and correspondence service
  • Full wardrobe provision and valet service

The Prussian Star · Eternal Passage · Berlin Terminus

Wilhelm

Global Holdings · Est. 1972 · Berlin, Germany

Operators of the world's only perpetual passenger locomotive. Wilhelm Global Holdings has connected the world's great cities by rail since 1952. The Prussian Star remains the pinnacle of that endeavour — a perpetual circuit, without pause, without terminus.

Conditions of Carriage Passenger Rights Train Layout
Our History

Built on Ambition

Wilhelm Global Holdings was established in Berlin in 1952 by Wilhelm von Vorbeck — engineer, entrepreneur, and one of the most consequential figures in post-war European infrastructure. In the years following the Second World War, the continent's transport networks lay in ruin. Bridges had been bombed, rail lines severed, entire junction cities reduced to rubble. The demand to move people and goods across Europe was extraordinary, and the supply to meet it was almost nothing.

Von Vorbeck had spent the war years as a civil engineer attached to the Reich's railway corps — not by conviction, but by conscription. What he observed gave him a precise and hard-won understanding of how rail networks could be built fast, maintained under pressure, and extended across hostile terrain. When peace came, he put that knowledge to use for himself.

Within a decade of its founding, Wilhelm Global Holdings had become one of the foremost heavy rail contractors on the continent, responsible for the reconstruction of the Rhineland freight corridors, the expansion of the Hamburg port rail approach, and early consultancy work on the Scandinavian northern lines. Von Vorbeck was not a man who stayed still. He was already thinking beyond reconstruction.

1952

Wilhelm Global Holdings registered in Berlin. Von Vorbeck secures first reconstruction contracts in the Rhineland, capitalising on the post-war demand for rapid rail infrastructure.

1957

Construction of the Prussian Star begins at the Bochum works. Von Vorbeck's vision: a single locomotive capable of sustained transcontinental operation at commercial passenger standards.

1963

The Prussian Star completes her maiden technical trials across the central European test corridor. First-class carriages begin fitment in Vienna and Antwerp workshops.

"The war taught me how quickly the world's arteries can be cut. I decided to build one that could not be."

— Wilhelm von Vorbeck · Founder · Berlin, 1965

The Refit

By the mid-1960s it had become clear that the Prussian Star's original configuration — designed for European long-haul — would need to be substantially enlarged if von Vorbeck's ambition of a true global circuit was to be realised. Between 1966 and 1969, the entire train underwent a comprehensive refit at the Bochum works.

New carriages were added across all classes. The locomotive was re-engined with a more powerful drive system capable of sustaining speeds across the varied gradients of Africa, Central Asia, and the Americas. Passenger capacity was extended to accommodate over two thousand travellers in formal classes, with provision for several hundred additional passengers in the rear cargo and residential sectors.

The Departure

On the fourteenth of March, 1972 — the twentieth anniversary of the company's founding — the Prussian Star departed Berlin on her first full global circuit. The platform at Berlin Central was crowded to capacity. Von Vorbeck watched from the observation car as the city receded.

She has not stopped since. The train completes her great circuit across eighty-one mainline cities, boarding passengers and taking on cargo at each waypoint before continuing without pause. The management of Wilhelm Global Holdings operates from Carriage Four and maintains full authority over all matters aboard.

81
Mainline Cities
82
Carriages
~2,500
Souls Aboard
1972
Year of Departure
The Passengers

The Prussian Star carries approximately two thousand passengers across her formal classes — 1st, 2nd, and 3rd — as well as a dedicated investor sector housing the company's principal backers in purpose-built residential carriages forward of the main passenger zones.

At the rear of the train, in the Tail sector, a further five hundred or so individuals travel under informal arrangements — stowaways, displaced persons, and those who boarded at waypoints without formal documentation. Their presence is acknowledged, managed, and regulated by the Security Force and the Judicial Department.

Wilhelm von Vorbeck

Born in Berlin in 1918, von Vorbeck studied civil engineering at the Technische Hochschule Berlin before the war intervened. He returned to civilian life in 1945 with seven years of railway engineering experience acquired under the most demanding conditions imaginable.

He founded Wilhelm Global Holdings at thirty-four, built the Prussian Star in his late thirties, and watched her depart on her maiden global circuit at fifty-four. He remains aboard, operating from Carriage Three — his private residence — and continues to oversee the company's direction personally.

The Eternal Circuit

Routes of the Prussian Star

Passenger Communications

The Telegraph Office

The Telegraph Office serves as the primary point of contact for passenger queries, complaints, hospitality requests, and welfare communications. All messages dispatched through this office are handled by the Head of Hospitality and Passenger Relations aboard the Prussian Star.

For enquiries regarding your passage, cabin assignment, dietary requirements, medical access, or any matter pertaining to your experience aboard the train, please direct your correspondence to the Head of Hospitality.

All communications are received during operational hours. Response time is typically within 24 hours of the next waypoint dispatch.

Head of Hospitality & Passenger Relations

Mert Atalar

Carriage 16 · Hospitality Division

Contact via Discord

@mertatalar2

Open Discord Server
Passenger Admissions

The Admissions Office

The Admissions Office is responsible for the review, approval, and processing of all passage applications submitted to Wilhelm Global Holdings. Every passenger aboard the Prussian Star has passed through this office, regardless of class or investor status.

All reservations are subject to review. The Head Administrator retains full discretion over boarding eligibility and may decline any application without obligation to provide cause. Decisions are final and not subject to appeal through civilian channels.

Required Documentation
  • — Valid travel documents (passport or state-issued permit)
  • — Proof of passage payment or Roblox community membership (3rd Class)
  • — Medical clearance certificate (recommended)
  • — Correspondence address for confirmation dispatch
  • — Declaration of special requirements (if applicable)
Head Administrator & Operations Manager

ClauClau

Carriage 4 · Administration Division

Contact via Discord

@clauclau.1

Open Discord Server
Media Relations

Press Enquiries

Wilhelm Global Holdings welcomes media engagement from accredited press organisations, documentary crews, and independent journalists. All press access to the Prussian Star is managed through our official Discord server and requires advance clearance from the Administration Office.

Press credentials do not exempt holders from the Conditions of Carriage. Media personnel aboard are subject to the same class restrictions as civilian passengers unless a specific access arrangement has been confirmed in writing by the Head Administrator.

Photography and filming within investor carriages, the engine sector, and the administration car is strictly prohibited at all times.

✉
Submit a Press Enquiry

All press enquiries are handled via our official Discord server. Open a ticket using the ticket tool to reach the media relations team. Include your outlet name, intended subject, and proposed access dates.

Open Ticket on Discord

Use the ticket tool in the server · Response within 48 hours

General Terms
Passenger Charter

Passenger Rights

As guaranteed by Wilhelm Global Holdings to all passengers aboard the Prussian Star, regardless of class. Effective from 14 March 1972.

How to raise a complaint

Any passenger wishing to raise a concern, grievance, or formal complaint must do so through the Hospitality Department in the first instance. The Head of Hospitality will endeavour to resolve the matter within 48 hours.

If the matter cannot be resolved by Hospitality, the passenger may submit a formal petition to the Administration Office (Carriage 4). The Head Administrator will review the petition and issue a binding determination.

Matters of law, discipline, or inter-passenger dispute are referred to the Judicial Department. Proceedings are conducted in the Judicial Car (Carriage 47) and presided over by the High Magistrate.

Wilhelm Global Holdings is committed to the dignity and welfare of every individual aboard the Prussian Star. These rights exist alongside — and do not supersede — the Conditions of Carriage. In the event of conflict between these rights and administrative necessity, the determination of the Head Administrator shall be final.
